steps.title: Test which point satisfies both inequalities
steps.content: A solution must satisfy both: $y \le x + 7$ AND $y \ge -2x - 1$. Substitute each choice into both inequalities.

steps.title: Test choices A through C
steps.content:
• Choice A $(-14,0)$: $0 \le -14+7$ becomes $0 \le -7$, which is false.
  • Choice B $(0,-14)$: $-14 \le 0+7$ is true, but $-14 \ge -2(0)-1$ becomes $-14 \ge -1$, which is false.
  • Choice C $(0,14)$: $14 \le 0+7$ becomes $14 \le 7$, which is false.

steps.title: Testing choice D (14, 0)
steps.content: First inequality: $0 \le 14 + 7 = 21$? Yes. Second inequality: $0 \ge -2(14) - 1 = -28 - 1 = -29$? Yes. Both inequalities are satisfied. That matches choice D — D is correct.
